cards.grammarNote: ذَهَبَ (dhahaba) is the base form of the past tense — 3rd person masculine singular. The pattern فَعَلَ (fa'ala) with vowels a-a-a is the most common Form I past tense pattern.
Adding ـتْ (-at) to the base form creates the 3rd person feminine singular past. The taa' marbuta ت is written with sukuun (ـتْ) at the end of a sentence.
The 1st person singular past is formed by adding ـتُ (-tu) to the verb stem. The stem vowel drops: ذَهَبَ → ذَهَبْتُ.
The 1st person plural past is formed by adding ـنَا (-na) to the verb stem.
قَالَ (qala) is an irregular verb — it has a long vowel ā in the stem. The root is ق-و-ل. In 1st person: قُلْتُ (qultu), the long vowel shortens and the vowel changes.
قُلْتُ (qultu) shows the stem change in hollow verbs: the long ā shortens and the vowel changes to u when a consonant suffix is added.
فَعَلَ (fa'ala) = he did/made. This word is also the Arabic grammatical template verb — the root ف-ع-ل represents any generic verb root in grammar explanations.
